reasoning: This is a pure copy kernel with zero arithmetic operations—it only reads from `in` and writes to `out`, yielding an arithmetic intensity of 0 FLOP/byte, far below the ridge point of 62 FLOP/byte. The kernel moves 2 × 256 MB of data (one read + one write) with no computation, so its throughput is entirely dictated by DRAM bandwidth. With 4096 blocks × 256 threads = 1M threads and a grid-stride loop, there are sufficient warps to saturate the memory subsystem, meaning DRAM utilization will be high (>50% of peak), confirming a memory-bound classification rather than latency-bound or occupancy-limited.
